seoTitle: How to Create Custom Templates in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to build a reusable Google Docs template with placeholder text, submit it to your Template Gallery, and force copy links for teams.
intro: Standardizing letterheads, proposals, or weekly agendas is much easier with a proper template setup rather than copy-pasting old files. This guide covers designing a master layout, submitting it to your organization's Template Gallery, and creating forced-copy links. It's for teams that want consistent documents without accidental overwrites.

steps.heading: Build your master layout
steps.body: Structure your base document with the formatting and sections you want to reuse, then add clear placeholder text or smart chips to mark future data fields.

steps.heading: Set sharing permissions
steps.body: Click File, hover over Share, and confirm your baseline view-only permissions so others can't accidentally edit the master file.

steps.heading: Submit to the Template Gallery
steps.body: From your Docs home dashboard, click Template Gallery, choose your organization's tab, and click Submit Template.

steps.heading: Select and categorize your file
steps.body: Click Select a Document to choose your master file from Drive, assign it an accurate category, and click Submit.

steps.heading: Create a force-copy link as a backup
steps.body: In your browser's address bar, replace 'edit' with 'copy' at the end of the document's URL so anyone opening that link is prompted to clone the file.

faq.question: What's the benefit of a force-copy link?
faq.answer: It automatically prompts collaborators to duplicate the document instead of editing your original master file.

faq.question: Can I use placeholder text instead of leaving fields blank?
faq.answer: Yes, clear placeholder chips or bracketed text help future users know exactly what to fill in.

faq.question: Who can submit a template to the gallery?
faq.answer: This typically depends on your organization's Workspace permissions, so check with your admin if the option isn't available.

faq.question: Will editing the master template affect copies people already made?
faq.answer: No, once a document is copied, it becomes an independent file unaffected by later changes to the master.
